raw_input:
[4.0/5] Very glittery I have a lot of orange-gold lip colors because I find them very flattering on me, so I was pretty confident I would like this. The color is beautiful and not too orange. While I like the gold too, the specks of glitter are larger and more obvious than I would like. Some gold is really fine and distributes as an even shimmer across your lips. With this, you can see the glitter speck here, the glitter speck there, the glitter speck falling a little outside your lip line that will probably end up somewhere else on your face. It has more of a youthful, party feel. I agree it's a color reminiscent of sunsets, suited for summer and fall. The case is possibly the most sophisticated I own though. It has a nice weight, and the magnetic closure feels good. It came in a box and in a sealed bag. I don't mind wearing this color, but I wouldn't buy it again. I think I like the case the best, but I'm not convinced the color or feel add up to make it worth $38. [5.0/5] Happy with it! I had never heard of this brand before and was also looking forward to trying this out because I feel like this shade wouldn't look right on me- I guess because I have a lot of redness in my face. So getting the opportunity to try it out allowed me to branch out and try a shade I would normally be hesitant to buy. I'm glad because I love it! I ended-up loving the shade and how it looks on me. It applies so smoothly and even feels moisturizing while wearing. I love the shimmer- it's enough to be noticeable and pretty but isn't too much. Happy with it! [5.0/5] Moisturizing & Comfortable, Good Coverage, Pretty Red-Nude w/ A Sparkly Coppery Finish On Me This Emilie Heathe Lip Atelier Lip Colour in Shimmering Burnt Orange (Public) comes in a premium tube with a magnetic lid. I don’t notice any scent while wearing this, but this has “flavor” that I can smell if I hold the lipstick under my nose. The formula glides on smoothly and has a comfortable, moisturizing feel. My lips don’t get dried out or irritated, and the color doesn’t bleed even though I have dry skin. This is semi sheer yet buildable and even one swipe gives me good coverage that wears well. This does have sparkles that I need to remove after wear.

This lipstick is really pretty. It looks sparkly and shimmery and has become a favorite for evening as it’s more glam than my subtle everyday pinks, yet it doesn’t look overdone or too intense. I wouldn’t normally choose an orange shade, but this turned out to be really flattering with my light skin with an olive hue. While “burnt orange” is a fair description of how it looks in the tube, on me this looks less orange and more like a reddish/nude color with a sparkly finish that’s coppery. I think natural lip color and how much is applied will affect the look. Love it. 5 stars. [5.0/5] Shimmery, long lasting First time I've heard of this brand so I was eager to try something new. I'm happy with the color as it's not too bold or dark but more natural. It's not a super soft lipstick, it takes a few passes to really get good coverage. It has a texture to it because of the shimmer it has. I like that it lasts really well throughout the day and doesn't come off on everything! Seems to be high quality and I like the way it looks. Very happy with it. [5.0/5] Totally 90s orange sparkle lips Such a gorgeous color that can work for everyone. It reminds me of the orange glowing sunset when the next day is supposed to be hot. The glitter in the lipstick is such an amazing addition to the already beautiful color. The gold really shines in the light without looking dated. Using this without liner as an ombre lip is great for those who want a more subdued look.
Even with ultra fair skin this shade doesn't look overdone because the pigmentation is nice and light. It seems to last really well even though it's more of a glossy hydrating formula. [4.0/5] Feels great, but does not last long I love the way my lips feel and look with this lipstick, but I took away one star because it does not stay put as long as I would like and needs touching up more frequently than I would like. It also requires a bit more caution as it will rub off and smear (on me anyway) more easily than my usual long-wearing brand. But the the feel is so moisturizing and the color flattering that sometimes it’s just worth it if I’m all dressed up and want to look my best! [3.0/5] It's nice, but... Very nice color. It's somewhere in the bronze family. It works with both pale and tanned skin. The lipstick feels a little gritty on my lips from the glitter. The case is cool although I'm not sure why the cover is magnetic - so I don't lose it? It's a very nice color, but I'm just not comfortable with the luxury price tag on this one. [5.0/5] My Lips Are So Striking!! The luscious beauty of this shimmering succulent orange brings out a daringness to my lips. It’s bold, depending on my fashion style, and yet ultra complimentary and tasteful. It also has amazing texture, plus it generously hydrates, while offering me beautiful, moisturized lips. [5.0/5] Shimmery Tangerine Color- perfect! This Emilie Heathe Lip Color is a really good product. The lip color is a great color, peachy orange with a very small particle shimmer dust with gold flecks. It is a great color. Not over the top. The shimmer isn’t tacky shimmer, it’s just a hint. I also love the magnetic top to the lipstick case. It snaps right back on with ease. Love the lipstick! [4.0/5] Love the idea I'm interested in purchasing other colors from the line because I LOVE the overall formula, but the shimmering burnt orange shade wasn't flattering on me. The orange base was pretty and built up nicely with additional layers, but the actual shimmer particles imparted somewhat of a gritty texture to my lips and looked almost *too* sparkly. I think I would prefer finer shimmer. But otherwise, the product was very moisturizing and lightweight on the lips. If I can find a more suitable shade, I think it would be a very convenient and glamorous on-the-go product! [5.0/5] Emilie Heathe’s Lipstick…fabulous and beautiful! Emilie Heathe’s Lipstick in Public/Shimmering Burnt orange is fabulous! This lipstick gives me the right “pop” of color I have been looking for and has beautiful little sparkles in it, making it perfect for both daytime and night time (or even special occasion use!) There is so much to love about this lipstick!

1. This lipstick glides on easily, is moisturizing, and really complements my fair/ivory (with pink undertones) skin color.
2. It feels creamy and luxurious, while not feeling heavy, and feels perfectly natural on my lips no matter how long I was wearing it. It did not look orange on me, considering the name (shimmering burnt orange), but was a very pretty shade of medium peach. Love it!
3. The packaging for the lipstick is extremely chic, being black and sleek. The lipstick tube has a lid that “locks” together with the base, making me feel like the lid is more secure in my purse than a conventional lid. I have not used a lipstick with this kind of packaging before, and it is a nice detail. It is all about the details!
4. The icing on the cake is that this lipstick is vegan, cruelty-free, made in the U.S.A, and packaging is recyclable and sustainably so you can feel good about using this product in so many ways!

Hope you get a chance to love this great lipstick by Emilie Heathe, as I much as I do! [4.0/5] GLITTERING burnt orange This review is for the Emilie Heathe - Lip Atelier Lip Color in the "Shimmering Burnt Orange" shade, which I believe is the only "shimmery" color offered at the time of this writing. It comes in a super-chic understated black box, and the tube itself is also black and square with a magnetic lid.

Calling the shade "Shimmering" is an understatement - this shade is GLITTERING. The pigment itself is a really pretty Sienna / rust color that's very flattering on my pale, warm-undertoned skin, and it has a LOT of coppery-gold sparkle. It glides on my lips smoothly and puts down a good amount of pigment (and glitter) in a single swipe. It does feel a little bit gritty from the glitter, but doesn't leave my lips feeling dry at all. It does have a little bit of a scent that smells floral to me.

My problem with this particular shade is that the pigment fades (as all lip colors do) throughout the day, but the glitter does NOT. The glitter just migrates outside of your lips and you end up with it all around your mouth. So I personally wouldn't order this shade again. However I like the formula overall, and the fact that it's made in the USA from clean ingredients, so I'm sure the other (non-glittery) colors are amazing and would be right up my alley! [4.0/5] Very sparkly [[VIDEOID:d6cdea0c18d5911b6ddf88504e4441c9]] I expected a subtle shimmer with this lipstick, but it actually gives off more of a glittery look. I don't like to draw that much attention to myself and the color wasn't quite right for me, so it was a bit much for me at first, but I've figured out how to apply it to best suit me. I found that if I apply a light layer of this over my usual lipstick it adds just a bit of deeper color and a shimmer to give it a nice new look. I also found that if I do just a light layer of only this lipstick the color is more subtle and suits me better. If you like a lot of sparkle, you will love this. It really leaves your lips shining in the sunlight. Occasionally some of the glitter flakes will land on other places on you too, but it isn't really bad about that. The lipstick itself is a really good lipstick. It glides on easily and evenly and feels moisturizing. You will notice more of the moisturizing feeling if you apply it heavily, however, so with my light application that feeling doesn't last long. The color doesn't seem to last any longer than any other lipstick, but I never feel like any of them last too long. The case of this lipstick is actually quite nice as well. It is very smooth and shiny and has a bit of a weighted feel to it. The lid is magnetic so as you go to close it up the lid snaps itself shut. I appreciate the lack of parabens, sulfates, etc and that it is cruelty free as well. Due to the fact that the current listed price of $42 for one stick of lipstick is quite above budget for me, I will not purchase this one again. However, if that weren't the case, I would be interested in trying some of the other shades. [5.0/5] Warm and bold color The Emilie Heathe brand is new to me and I was glad to try the lip atelier. It has a great presentation, looks really good, and is long-lasting. I like that the ingredients are cruelty-free, clean, and sustainable. The color is a rich bold maroon that looks very flattering and goes on very sheer. It is smudge-proof and leaves my lips moisturized. [5.0/5] Exactly as described This is exactly as described and pictured! Two thumbs up!

response:
Color pretty, orange-gold, remind of sunset. Work on pale skin, tanned skin, fair skin, olive skin. Look less orange on some, more red-nude or peach on others. Shimmer there, but glitter big and visible on some lips, migrate outside lip line throughout day. Few say shimmer fine and subtle. Most like shimmer, some find gritty texture from glitter particles.

Formula smooth, moisturizing, comfortable. Build coverage with more layers. Some say last well, one say fade fast, need touch up. Glitter outlast color, end up around mouth.

Case fancy, black, square, magnetic lid, weighted feel. Come in box, sealed bag. Most like case very much.

Brand vegan, cruelty-free, made in USA, packaging recyclable.

Price around $38-42. Some think too much for lipstick. Most happy with product, many want try other shades from brand.
